funding_amount: GTO Grow covers 25% of first-year salary and 12.5% of second-year salary; GTO Innovate covers 30% of first-year salary and 15% of second-year salary; both add 10% in year 1 for eligible diversity hires

deadline_or_intake: applications accepted on an ongoing basis; approved projects must be completed within three years

funding_amount: rebate against eligible direct project costs; eligible project must have at least $350,000 in Nova Scotia investment and rebate applies to up to $15,000,000 in eligible project costs

deadline_or_intake: annual application for up to 3 years from source text

eligible_projects: provincial corporate income tax relief for new small businesses during the first three taxation years after incorporation

deadline_or_intake: project approval required; issued per taxation year

deadline_or_intake: Part A application before principal photography or key animation; Part B no later than 30 months after tax year end

eligible_projects: digital animation productions with eligible Nova Scotia labour, overhead, and remuneration expenditures

deadline_or_intake: optional Part A eligibility application available; mandatory Part B tax-credit-certificate application due no later than 30 months after the end of the relevant tax year

The page states Part B applications are due no later than 30 months after the end of the tax year in which qualifying expenditures were made.
funding_amount: refundable tax credit equal to the lesser of 50% of qualifying expenditures or 25% of total expenditures, with regional bonus up to 60% of qualifying expenditures or 30% of total expenditures and marketing/distribution credit up to $100,000 per product
program_name: Nova Scotia Digital Media Tax Credit

deadline_or_intake: available until March 2, 2032; eligible businesses must obtain a Certificate of Registration before issuing shares and submit investor data after the share issue for tax receipts
eligible_applicants: Nova Scotia corporations and co-operatives, including community economic development corporations and co-operatives, that meet active-business, size, payroll, and share-structure criteria

funding_amount: investor tax credit of 35% of eligible CEDIF investment up to a $50,000 annual investment, for a maximum annual credit of $17,500; additional 20% and 10% credits may apply after required holding periods
program_name: Nova Scotia Equity Tax Credit for Community Economic Development Investment Funds

deadline_or_intake: eligibility and tax credit certificate process through Nova Scotia film tax rules
eligible_applicants: taxable Canadian corporations with a permanent establishment in Nova Scotia that primarily carry on a film or video production business and pay at least 25% of salaries to Nova Scotians
eligible_projects: eligible film and video productions intended for television, cinema, videotape, or non-theatrical production in qualifying genres

funding_amount: base rates include 50% of eligible salaries or 25% of total production costs, with additional incentive rates available under source rules
program_name: Nova Scotia Film Industry Tax Credit
provider: Nova Scotia Department of Finance and Treasury Board

deadline_or_intake: application and submission materials must be received before principal photography starts
eligible_applicants: organizations with a permanent establishment in Nova Scotia that are primarily engaged in creating film or video productions for public viewing
eligible_projects: screen-based content creation with at least $25,000 in eligible Nova Scotia spend, including eligible labour, goods, and services costs

funding_amount: base incentive of 26% of eligible Nova Scotia costs for Stream I and 25% for Stream II, with additional rural, long-shoot, Nova Scotia content, and distant-location incentives where eligible
program_name: Nova Scotia Film and Television Production Incentive Fund
provider: Government of Nova Scotia Department of Communities, Culture, Tourism and Heritage

deadline_or_intake: application for certificate of registration required before eligible investments

eligible_projects: equity capital for research, development, or commercialization of new technology, new products, or new processes

funding_amount: 35% individual investor credit; 45% in oceans technology and life sciences sectors; 15% corporate investor credit up to $500,000 annual corporate investment amount
program_name: Nova Scotia Innovation Equity Tax Credit

deadline_or_intake: applications open until March 15, 2027 at 2:00 PM AST
eligible_applicants: registered Nova Scotia Loyal producers whose business is not in default under other Invest Nova Scotia, Province of Nova Scotia, or provincial-agency financial assistance programs
eligible_projects: labelling and packaging changes that integrate the Nova Scotia Loyal logo, including graphic design, labelling dies, and first print runs of labels or packaging for locally made products
evidence: Official Invest Nova Scotia source states the Nova Scotia Loyal Producer Labelling Program provides financial assistance for producers to purchase labelling products and services.
Official source states registered Nova Scotia Loyal producers can receive a rebate of 70% of eligible expenditures up to a lifetime maximum of $3,000 CAD.
funding_amount: 70% rebate on eligible labelling and packaging expenditures up to a lifetime maximum of $3,000 CAD, excluding GST/HST

deadline_or_intake: claimed with Nova Scotia T2 Schedule 340 alongside federal SR&ED claim
eligible_applicants: Nova Scotia corporations incurring qualified scientific research and experimental development expenditures in Nova Scotia
eligible_projects: qualified SR&ED expenditures made in Nova Scotia that are eligible for the federal SR&ED tax credit

deadline_or_intake: announced July 22, 2025; funded projects must be completed by January 2027; Penny should verify Perennia intake mechanics before recommending submission
eligible_applicants: Nova Scotia agriculture and seafood companies proposing large-scale growth, productivity, technology, business-model, or market-development projects
eligible_projects: large-scale projects that boost productivity, help businesses expand, adopt technology, change business practices, or find new markets for products

The release states the province is providing $4.71 million, funded projects must be completed by January 2027, and Perennia manages the fund.
funding_amount: $4.71 million provincial fund for agriculture and seafood strategic investment projects
program_name: Nova Scotia Seafood and Agriculture Strategic Investment Fund
provider: Government of Nova Scotia Departments of Agriculture and Fisheries and Aquaculture

deadline_or_intake: authorized raise and tax credit certificate applications are required; tax credit certificate application due within 6 months after the qualifying fund's authorized raise expires
eligible_applicants: individual and corporate investors investing in qualifying venture capital funds, and qualifying venture capital funds investing in eligible small and medium corporations within Atlantic Canada

Official source states rebates are equivalent to between 5% and 10% of gross payroll and that projects should create at least 20 incremental jobs in Nova Scotia.
funding_amount: performance rebate equal to 5% to 10% of eligible gross payroll, paid annually after the company meets employment, payroll, and salary targets, up to an approved amount over a typical five-year term

deadline_or_intake: program opened April 1, 2026; applications due June 30, 2026; activity period April 1, 2026 to February 28, 2027; claim deadline February 28, 2027
eligible_applicants: Nova Scotia registered farms, Mi'kmaq conducting farming activities in Mi'kmaw communities, agri-businesses, and agricultural industry associations that meet program criteria
eligible_projects: consultant services, business plans, succession plans, feasibility studies, emergency preparedness plans, data analysis, engineering plans, process improvement plans, food safety plans, farm safety plans, and eligible translation services

funding_amount: case-by-case wage incentive and related funding based on employment type, wage, employee skill level, unemployment duration, and priority-sector factors

evidence: Official Nova Scotia source states the program supports purchase of equipment and technology for automation, efficiency, production capacity, and quality improvements.

eligible_projects: value-added equipment that increases local food production, food security, and sustainability for farms and communities
evidence: Official Nova Scotia source states the program supports value-added businesses and helps increase local food production.

deadline_or_intake: open for applications twice per year; current intake period listed on program page

deadline_or_intake: applications may be submitted on or before April 15, August 15 if funding is available, or December 15 if funding is available; applications are assessed after each deadline through a competitive process
eligible_applicants: Nunavut licensed tourism outfitters, licensed tourist establishments that qualify as Nunavut small businesses, eligible tourism/cultural organizations, municipalities, artists or artist groups, and societies or not-for-profits with tourism, cultural, or arts objectives
eligible_projects: tourism and cultural industry development, product development and marketing, business skills training for tourism businesses, small business tourism planning, marketing tourism activities, cultural tourism, and small asset improvements

funding_amount: Schedule A: $1,000 to $35,000 per project per year; Schedule B: $5,000 to $100,000 per project per year; Schedule C: $25,000 to $100,000 per project per year, with applicant contribution requirements generally 10% for artists and 20% for other non-hamlet applicants

deadline_or_intake: applications through current NFDC online form

funding_amount: up to three grants of $7,500 each per project phase

deadline_or_intake: normally once per fiscal year; multi-year conditional approvals possible subject to funds
eligible_applicants: Nunavut businesses, residents, and organizations seeking entrepreneurship, business aftercare, or tourism safety and risk management support
eligible_projects: business aftercare programs, entrepreneurship capacity, business capacity support, and tourism safety and risk management services

deadline_or_intake: submit completed application with business plan, proposal, financial information, project costs, funding sources, and supporting documentation to the appropriate regional Economic Development and Transportation contact; no fixed deadline stated on official application form
eligible_applicants: Nunavut business applicants including corporations, cooperatives, partnerships, sole proprietorships, and businesses to be incorporated, across manufacturing, renewable resource, non-renewable resource, tourism/hospitality, cultural industries, and other sectors

evidence: Official NFDC source states the Nunavut Spend Incentive Program awards production companies a rebate on eligible production goods and services purchased and consumed in Nunavut.

eligible_applicants: Nunavut businesses and Nunavut small businesses, including resident-owned corporations, cooperatives, sole proprietorships, partnerships, societies, businesses in the NNI directory, artists, craftspersons, performing artists, renewable-resource harvesters, and small businesses with less than $500,000 in annual gross sales or fewer than 10 employees
eligible_projects: business planning, feasibility and engineering studies, pre-establishment or expansion costs, professional services, demonstration or pilot projects, technology/equipment/materials, capital assets, start-up or expansion working capital gaps, market development, product launch, recovery planning, entrepreneur development, business aftercare, tourism safety, tools, equipment, and small business operating expenses

deadline_or_intake: monthly intakes until annual funds are allocated

deadline_or_intake: final claims are available through latest tax returns for tax years ending March 31, 2025 or earlier; rebates are not available for year ends after March 31, 2025

deadline_or_intake: applications accepted monthly by 4:30 pm on the first Friday of each month, or the next business day if the first Friday is a statutory holiday; funding decisions generally within 6 weeks of the application deadline
eligible_applicants: Yukon commercial farm or agricultural businesses, agri-food processors, other agri-businesses, First Nation businesses, new entrants and small businesses, non-profit organizations, governments, institutions, and research bodies meeting Sustainable CAP criteria
